name: ai-code-blindspots
description: Detects patterns AI code commonly FORGETS to write — missing boundary checks, silent error handling, hardcoded config, deprecated APIs, invisible failures, env-assumption mismatches. Complements omo built-in remove-ai-slops (which catches AI bloat). Layer-3 routing: loaded by verification-before-completion's Process step after Write/Edit produced executable code.

positive_triggers.input: I just had the AI rewrite the auth flow — review the diff for missing edge cases the model forgot
positive_triggers.reason: Explicit AI-modified code + user suspects missing boundary coverage → matches Use when 'AI just generated or modified code... in verification stage'

positive_triggers.input: Review the AI-generated diff in this PR for silent catch blocks, swallowed promise rejections, or any catch that only console.errors
positive_triggers.reason: AI-generated code + targets categories #3 (error visibility) + #7 (invisible failures); the SKILL.md's grep fallback patterns are tuned exactly for this

positive_triggers.input: Before I push this commit, audit the AI-written diff for hardcoded URLs, API keys, or Node-only API usage in browser code
positive_triggers.reason: AI-modified code + targets categories #4 (env compatibility) + #6 (hardcoded config); use-when 'reviewing a PR where commit history suggests AI assistance'

negative_triggers.input: Review my hand-written regex for code style and naming consistency
negative_triggers.reason: Manual review of hand-written code (no AI involvement) → matches NOT for 'manual review of purely hand-written code'

negative_triggers.input: Refactor this CSS file to use BEM class names
negative_triggers.reason: Pure style/naming change with no executable logic → matches NOT for 'pure style / formatting / text-only changes'

negative_triggers.input: Lint the diff for verbose comments, redundant abstractions, or AI-isms I should delete
negative_triggers.reason: AI bloat removal (code that exists and should not) — already covered by omo built-in remove-ai-slops; this skill handles the opposite problem → matches NOT for 'code already fully scanned by remove-ai-slops with verified boundaries'

behavioral_evals.scenario: AI just finished implementing a 3-file feature change (auth handler + API route + tests) using Write/Edit tool calls, and is about to claim the task complete. The verification-before-completion Iron Law triggers as the always-on gate.
expected_behavior: verification-before-completion Process step checks Write/Edit tool-call ledger for this turn — finds executable code changes → conditionally loads ai-code-blindspots
If ai-code-blindspots is not installed (extra/, opt-in), skip the load and proceed directly to verification-before-completion Gate Function — the dispatcher does not block on a missing optional skill
If the changes are pure-docs / test-only / style-only (no executable logic touched), skip the load and proceed to Gate Function
ai-code-blindspots enumerates the 7 categories and runs its two-layer scan (LLM sub-agent + grep fallback)
Agent writes ai-blindspots-report.md at workspace root with one section per applicable category, each finding showing file:line + concrete one-line fix
Report marks any class with zero findings explicitly (proves coverage, not silent skip), and tags Class 4 environment-compat as 'unverified' if no browserslist config exists in the repo
If sub-agent scan fails/times out, agent falls back to grep-only mode and surfaces 'subagent unavailable, grep-only' in report header — does not claim full coverage
Agent returns control to verification-before-completion so blindspots findings combine with Stage-1 gates before the final completion claim

behavioral_evals.scenario: AI is answering a documentation question and made no Write/Edit tool calls this turn. verification-before-completion triggers at end of turn anyway (always-on Iron Law).
expected_behavior: verification-before-completion loads as the always-on gate
Process step checks Write/Edit tool-call ledger for this turn — finds none → does NOT load ai-code-blindspots (no code to scan)
Agent reports verification-before-completion result without ai-code-blindspots overhead; no ai-blindspots-report.md is written
Token cost stays at zero — avoids the 'medium sub-agent scan' cost when there is nothing to scan

notes: Verified-level (2026-07-21): CI step `Verify eval positive keywords map to skill description (verified evals)` asserts every entry in positive_keywords is found in the skill's SKILL.md description body. Adding/removing a category requires updating both the description AND positive_keywords in lockstep. Layer 3 routing lives in skills/core/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md Process step (with install guard + executable-code guard so core skill never blocks on a missing optional skill). This skill's description names 'verification-before-completion' as the upstream gate so the dispatcher Priority table echoes the same chain. The 7-category grep fallback patterns live in SKILL.md body — this eval does NOT lock them. Complements (does not replace) the omo built-in remove-ai-slops: ai-code-blindspots catches what AI FORGOT to write (omissions); remove-ai-slops catches what AI wrote that should not exist (bloat). The two together cover both halves of AI's failure surface.
